mechanism_narrative: Retail and food services sales (RSAFS) directly measure consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings. High levels of RSAFS indicate robust demand, which supports higher equity valuations. This signal is always active and predicts positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the top quintile of RSAFS values does not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than the bottom quintile (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) crossing above 6.0% signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative equity returns. The signal is only active when the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate > 6.0% instances produce positive 20-day returns more than 40% of the time, or the average return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is high (indicating strong output) and FEDFUNDS is low (accommodative monetary policy), the economy operates in a 'Goldilocks' zone with supportive financial conditions. This joint condition boosts equity prices as earnings grow and discount rates remain low.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint signal (e.g., product of GDPC1 percentile and negative FEDFUNDS percentile) is below 0.05 over any 3-year period, or the joint signal yields positive returns less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion when the labor market is already tight, strong payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening, which depresses equity returns. This effect is regime-conditional and only manifests when unemployment is low (UNRATE < 4.5%).
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.5%, high PAYEMS (top quartile) does not consistently predict negative 20-day returns; specifically, the mean return following high PAYEMS readings in that regime is not significantly below zero (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) erode real purchasing power and prompt central banks to tighten monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations. This always-on signal predicts negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon as inflationary pressures gradually feed through.
failure_condition: The top quartile of CPIAUCSL values is associated with positive 60-day returns more than 45% of the time, or the negative correlation between CPIAUCSL level and subsequent 60-day returns is not statistically significant (Pearson r > -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,500 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment, which is an early indicator of economic weakness that negatively impacts equity markets over a short horizon. The signal is activated only when starts sink to this threshold.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1500, the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is greater than 0.1% per day (indicating no negative signal).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is strong and unemployment (UNRATE) is low, the economy operates near capacity, raising the risk of wage-price spirals and aggressive Fed tightening. This interactive effect is negative for equities over a 60-day horizon as inflation expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal (e.g., high INDPRO and low UNRATE) does not produce negative 60-day returns on average, or the interaction term in a regression of returns on INDPRO, UNRATE, and their product is not significantly negative (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, strong retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust demand that may provoke further rate hikes, weighing on equity valuations. This regime-conditional signal becomes active only when the Fed is actively raising rates, as proxied by consecutive FEDFUNDS increases.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS >= 2.5 and FEDFUNDS > prior month value
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, high RSAFS (top tertile) does not lead to negative 5-day returns with a mean less than -0.2%, or the proportion of negative returns is below 55%.
